title: Dyeable Dice
body:
This datapack adds usable dice to Minecraft!

## Crafting Dice
Dice are very easy to craft - place a bone in a stonecutter, and select which die you want to craft. There are 6 dice values available: d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, and d20.  
![Dice crafting in a Stonecutter](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3e453d1dcea25791401f65c659664267f5c449cf.png)  

## Using Dice
Dice can be thrown by right clicking them. They will show their current value as a name above the item. When they hit the ground and stop moving, they'll rest on a final value. This value will also be whispered in chat to the person who initially rolled the die:  
![A die thrown normally](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b34c0ee47c873b096ff9431326c82a19fd32ab58.png)  

To roll in secret, a player can hold sneak while throwing the die. The final roll value will still be whispered to them in chat, but will not appear as a name above the item:  
![A die thrown while sneaking](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a0d3346dacf4dad0f4fff00854fe2fe5914d7a44.png)

Dice can also be rolled by firing them from a dispenser! In this case, no roll value will be announced in chat, but the roll value will appear as a name above the item:  
![A die rolled by a dispenser](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/853a9494ff04664d66d9c4dbc7cfd512fa184b12.png)

Dice can't be picked up from the ground while they are still rolling!

While dice are not stackable normally, you can hold up to 16 in a bundle for easier storage or transportation:  
![A bundle full of dice](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/2697ede78df1382df77bc0d0b940ecfa800f354b.png)

## Customizing Dice  
### Dice Color
Dice are dyeable in the same way that leather armor is - simply craft together with dyes and mix until the desired color is obtained. For help figuring out the correct dyes to obtain the desired value, the Minecraft Wiki's [Armor Color Calculator](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Calculators/Armor_color) is very helpful!

To clean a die, toss it into a water cauldron. Its dye color will be removed and it will return to its default off-white bone color.

### Dice Trims
There are 4 trim patterns available for dice:

**Outline Trim**  
This trim is crafted in a smithing table using your die, any trim material, and an iron fence.
It highlights the edges of the die:  
![Red dice with a Netherite Outline Trim](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/45cf03ff6d481c6eaf5249c97af0be85607a1775.png)  

**Corner Trim**  
This trim is crafted in a smithing table using your die, any trim material, and a flint. It highlights the corners of the die:  
![Blue dice with Gold Corner Trim](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/894a15777b395aa5db2a5c0d3c0b56680602e430.png)  

**Creeper Trim**  
This trim is crafted in a smithing table using your die, any trim material, and a creeper head. It shows a creeper face on the center of the die:  
![White dice with Amethyst Creeper Trim](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0cead91e9f35bd4bb220d7213f34e81cae50bb3b.png)  

**Grassy Trim**  
This trim is crafted in a smithing table using your die, any trim material, and a grass block. It shows a grassy design on the top half of the die:  
![Brown dice with Emerald Grassy Trim](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/35d541f9d980c835179a6d182af514ff6720cf44.png)  

### Glint  
If you want the ultimate flex for your custom die, it can be crafted with a Nether Star to gain an enchantment glint effect:  
![Enchantment Glint Recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6ebd7e20e3a70a706ae104e09dfbee5b1e21086f.png)

### Finalization  
Once you are satisfied with your die's Color and Trim, you can finalize it by crafting it with a glass block. This will lock the trim, and prevent the dye from being changed or washed off:  
![Dice Lock Recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b3a69b38a1120b2c283b0c7b47840e8ec73c16f1.png)
